title: Nursery Rhyme. LXXVII. Tales.
content:
The man in the moon,
            Came tumbling down,
        And ask'd his way to Norwich,
            He went by the south,
            And burnt his mouth
        With supping cold pease-porridge.
meaning:
"Nursery Rhyme. LXXVII. Tales." is a quintessential example of Unknown's signature style...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
